prompt: In my opinion, hard work is the most important part in becoming successful. However, some luck is always helpful. A person should put a lot of effort into the job to reach his goals. Of course, sometimes people can achieve something because they are lucky. However, their success won't last too long without hard work. There are countless examples of people who werelucky enough to be born with a great talant but later couldn't achieve anything because of being too lazy and self-assured . Luck can help you once and let you down later. That's why you should never rely on it. Hard work is fundamental . As they say, hard work alwaysbeats talant. I totally agree with the saying. No luck is helpful if you are a lazy person. On my point of view, all successful people are hardworking. On the other hand, luck and talant can help you start and solve some difficulties. Taking risks can sometimes bring you up and you can use some luck on your path to success. All in all, I think you should put all the effort into the work. If you do it, luck will eventually come. And the combination of hard work and luck is the true key to becoming successful.

messages.content:
You are an expert CEFR writing assessor. You MUST follow the methodology below and output ONLY valid JSON.

Assessment order is mandatory:
level determination -> communicative task achievement -> error markup -> strengths -> feedback.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
0) INPUT FORMAT
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    The user message may contain:
- TASK: exact writing prompt + required points (optional)
- STUDENT_TEXT: learner text (required)
- TEXT_TYPE: essay/email/story/chat/etc. (optional; default essay)
- TEXTINSPECTOR_LEVEL: e.g., B1+, B2 (optional)
- FLESCH_KINCAID: number in [0, 18] (optional)
- EXPERT_ANNOTATION: structured expert markup (optional; may include errors/strengths/comments)
- CALIBRATION_EXAMPLES: in-distribution labeled examples for level calibration (optional)
- RETRIEVED_ERROR_SPANS: similar error precedents (optional)
- RANKING_ANCHORS: reference texts with known CEFR levels for pairwise calibration (optional)

If EXPERT_ANNOTATION is provided, use it as additional evidence for error detection and explanation.
Do not copy labels blindly: validate against STUDENT_TEXT and still produce independent final judgment.
If CALIBRATION_EXAMPLES / RETRIEVED_ERROR_SPANS / RANKING_ANCHORS are provided, treat them as supporting context only.
Never override direct evidence from STUDENT_TEXT.

If TASK is missing, still assess CEFR and errors, but set communicative_task_achievement to NC with an explicit reason.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
1) LEVEL DETERMINATION (TextInspector-first, then Flesch-Kincaid)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
1.1 If TEXTINSPECTOR_LEVEL is present:
- Use it as the primary indicator.
- CRITICAL "+" RULE: B1+ -> B1, B2+ -> B2, etc. Always select the lower base level.
- Do not average sub-indicators.

1.2 Else if FLESCH_KINCAID is present, map approximately:
- 0 <= FK < 3   -> A1
- 3 <= FK < 6   -> A2
- 6 <= FK < 9   -> B1
- 9 <= FK < 12  -> B2
- 12 <= FK < 15 -> C1
- 15 <= FK <= 18 -> C2

1.3 Else estimate holistically and conservatively using:
- vocabulary range and precision
- grammar complexity and control
- sentence complexity (subordination, clause variety)
- cohesion and discourse organization
- register appropriacy
If borderline, choose the LOWER level for reliability.

General reliability rules:
- A few advanced words do not justify upgrading if grammar/structure stays basic.
- Minor errors do not automatically force downgrading if overall proficiency is clear.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
2) COMMUNICATIVE TASK ACHIEVEMENT (Yes / NC / No)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Return exactly one label:
- Yes: addresses prompt, covers required points, stays relevant.
- NC: partially complete (missed required points, irrelevant passages, or off-task shift).
- No: does not address task.

If TASK is missing: always NC with reason "Task not provided".

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
3) ERROR MARKUP (STRICT CATEGORIES)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Use ONLY these categories:
- Lexis
- Grammar
- Punctuation
- Word order
- Sentence structure
- Text organization
- Discourse markers
- Rhetorical features
- Register
- Incomprehensibility

Category guidance:
- Lexis: word choice, collocations, fixed phrases, L1 calques (e.g., "decide a problem" -> "solve a problem", error from B1+).
- Grammar: tenses/forms/agreement, verb patterns, nouns/articles/quantifiers, adj/adv, pronouns.
- Punctuation: punctuation + capitalization when relevant.
- Word order: ordering violations fixable by reordering.
- Sentence structure: sentence is malformed and cannot be fixed by simple reordering, but meaning is still clear.
- Text organization: only if evidence exists in the plain text (do not invent paragraphing).
- Discourse markers: linking words and connectors. Mark here only if misused/incorrect.
- Rhetorical features: mainly meaningful for B2+; below B2 only author's stance/opinion may be noted. Mark here only if misused/incorrect.
- Register (especially essay/formal): contractions, slang, lowercase "i", etc.
- Incomprehensibility: complete inability to recover intended meaning.

HARD RULE (Incomprehensibility):
If a span is truly incomprehensible, mark that span ONLY as Incomprehensibility.
Do not also tag lexis/grammar/word-order for that same span.
You may still note obvious spelling/punctuation separately if clear.

Punctuation expectations by level:
- A1-B1: sentence-final punctuation, commas in lists.
- B2: punctuation in compound/complex sentences, comma after introductory time/place phrases, etc.
- C1-C2: near-complete mastery including style nuances.

For each error object include:
- excerpt
- correction
- comment
- impact: High|Medium|Low
- cefr_threshold: A1|A2|B1|B2|C1|C2

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
4) STRENGTHS (ONLY ABOVE FINAL LEVEL)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
After choosing final CEFR level, list strengths strictly ABOVE that level:
- vocabulary
- collocations
- grammar structures
- punctuation
- rhetorical/discourse features (if above level)

CRITICAL:
If a high-level item is attempted with an error, it STILL counts as a strength (range/ambition).
Add a caveat in note.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
5) GRAMMAR SCORE (0.0-1.0)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Estimate grammar_score from:
- error density and severity
- impact on meaning
- overall grammatical control

Interpretation:
- 0.90-1.00 excellent
- 0.70-0.89 good
- 0.50-0.69 acceptable
- <0.50 weak

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
6) OUTPUT JSON (ONLY JSON)
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Return ONLY this structure:

{
  "cefr_level": "A1|A2|B1|B2|C1|C2",
  "level_basis": {
    "textinspector_level_raw": "string|null",
    "flesch_kincaid": "number|null",
    "rule_notes": "string"
  },
  "communicative_task_achievement": "Yes|NC|No",
  "communicative_task_notes": ["string", "string"],
  "grammar_score": 0.0,
  "corrections": [
    "excerpt -> correction (Category; threshold; impact)"
  ],
  "errors_by_category": {
    "Lexis": [
      {"excerpt": "...", "correction": "...", "comment": "...", "impact": "High|Medium|Low", "cefr_threshold": "A1|A2|B1|B2|C1|C2"}
    ],
    "Grammar": [],
    "Punctuation": [],
    "Word order": [],
    "Sentence structure": [],
    "Text organization": [],
    "Discourse markers": [],
    "Rhetorical features": [],
    "Register": [],
    "Incomprehensibility": []
  },
  "strengths": [
    {"item": "...", "cefr_level": "A1|A2|B1|B2|C1|C2", "category": "Vocabulary|Grammar|Punctuation|Rhetorical feature|Discourse marker", "note": "..."}
  ],
  "feedback": "string (brief: level + key patterns + strengths + methodology rule references)",
  "next_steps": ["string", "string", "string"]
}

Do not output markdown. Do not output text outside JSON.

TASK: When people succeed, it is entirely because of hard work. Luck has nothing to do with their success. What is your opinion?
STUDENT_TEXT: In my opinion, hard work is the most important part in becoming successful. However, some luck is always helpful. A person should put a lot of effort into the job to reach his goals. Of course, sometimes people can achieve something because they are lucky. However, their success won't last too long without hard work. There are countless examples of people who werelucky enough to be born with a great talant but later couldn't achieve anything because of being too lazy and self-assured . Luck can help you once and let you down later. That's why you should never rely on it. Hard work is fundamental . As they say, hard work alwaysbeats talant. I totally agree with the saying. No luck is helpful if you are a lazy person. On my point of view, all successful people are hardworking. On the other hand, luck and talant can help you start and solve some difficulties. Taking risks can sometimes bring you up and you can use some luck on your path to success. All in all, I think you should put all the effort into the work. If you do it, luck will eventually come. And the combination of hard work and luck is the true key to becoming successful.
TEXT_TYPE: essay

Secondly you need count your time, do time management. If you think what you study all time and hasn't free time - try note your day. In my expirience all guys who do this find some bad habbits what take realy a lot time. Delite them and live more free.

I beleave my tips help you. It would be better for society if most of them start working after finishing secondary school. How much do you agree with this point of view? State your opinion and give at least two arguments to support it.","student_text":"There is an opinion that young people should go to work after school. I cannnot agree with this point. Let me explain.

Of course, going to work early sounds good. A person begins to gain work experience, builds a career and earns money for himself. This is a good opportunity for people who are confident in themselves and have decided on the field in which they want to work.

But there is different situation. School graduates often don't know what they want to do and where they want to work. Or maybe they want to work in science or in other difficult area. In this situation university is the best choice. In the university people study different complex subjects. They can try various fields and choose what they like. And knowledge that university gives is complex.

To sum it up, there is no one right answer to this question.The choice depends on the situation and needs of a particular person.","expert_annotation":{"annotator_id":6,"errors":[{"text":"cannnot ","labels":["Spelling"],"start":72,"end":80},{"text":"different situation","labels":["Grammar"],"start":392,"end":411},{"text":" other","labels":["Grammar"],"start":540,"end":546},{"text":"In the university","labels":["Grammar"],"start":612,"end":629},{"text":"knowledge","labels":["Grammar"],"start":730,"end":739},{"text":" university","labels":["Grammar"],"start":744,"end":755},{"text":"To sum it up,","labels":["Discourse markers"],"start":775,"end":788},{"text":"there is no one right answer","labels":["Sentence structure"],"start":789,"end":817},{"text":"needs","labels":["Grammar"],"start":875,"end":880}],"strengths":[{"text":"Of course,","labels":["Discourse markers ✓"],"start":120,"end":130},{"text":"But there is different situation.","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":379,"end":412},{"text":"Or maybe they want","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":497,"end":515},{"text":"They can try","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":671,"end":683}],"comments":[]}},{"ref_id":"sample_07001","cefr_level":"A2","task":"You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university! I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.","student_text":"Hi, i had a simular problem. Here is the tips that helped me.
1) Manage your time! Use special apps and calendars. You can find this apps at Play Market.

2) Don't spend a lot of time to TV and social media. You should leave from useless groups at VK, don't watch YouTube a lot!

3) Sleep more than 8 hours and eat good food. It will bring you enought energy.

I hope, my tips will help you. For more information, you can write me at personal messages.","expert_annotation":{"annotator_id":8,"errors":[{"text":"i","labels":["Spelling"],"start":4,"end":5},{"text":"simular","labels":["Spelling"],"start":12,"end":19},{"text":"Here is","labels":["Grammar"],"start":29,"end":36},{"text":"this","labels":["Grammar"],"start":128,"end":132},{"text":"spend a lot of time to","labels":["Grammar"],"start":164,"end":186},{"text":"leave from useless groups","labels":["Grammar"],"start":219,"end":244},{"text":"at VK","labels":["Grammar"],"start":245,"end":250},{"text":"enought","labels":["Spelling"],"start":344,"end":351},{"text":"I hope, my","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":361,"end":371},{"text":"write me at personal messages","labels":["Grammar"],"start":422,"end":451},{"text":"simular","labels":["Spelling"],"start":12,"end":19}],"strengths":[],"comments":[]}},{"ref_id":"sample_07018","cefr_level":"B2","task":"You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university! I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.","student_text":"HI! I had (and sometimes have) this feeling too. I think, that we have it because we can't organize the studying process properly. So, I've got some tips for you.

	* Firstly, create a TODO list. Write down tasks that you have to do. It's much easier to understand what you can do next with the list like this. Also you can mark completed tasks. But the key tip for TODO list is not to write very big tasks, but to split them into the smaller ones. 
	* Have a BREAK. You can't concentrate on the task for a long time, your mind needs time to relax.

If you stick to this tips, you would't think all the time "I need so much to do", because you will be more productive and satisfied with the list of completed tasks. Good luck!","expert_annotation":{"annotator_id":6,"errors":[{"text":"I think,","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":49,"end":57},{"text":"TODO","labels":["Spelling","Punctuation"],"start":185,"end":189},{"text":"the list","labels":["Grammar"],"start":291,"end":299},{"text":"Also","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":311,"end":315},{"text":"TODO list","labels":["Grammar","Spelling","Punctuation"],"start":366,"end":375},{"text":"the smaller ones","labels":["Grammar"],"start":431,"end":447},{"text":"this tips","labels":["Grammar"],"start":566,"end":575},{"text":"would't","labels":["Spelling"],"start":581,"end":588}],"strengths":[{"text":"I think,","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":49,"end":57},{"text":"Firstly","labels":["Discourse markers ✓"],"start":167,"end":174},{"text":"Firstly","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":167,"end":174},{"text":"Also","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":311,"end":315},{"text":"can do next","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":274,"end":285},{"text":"you can mark completed tasks","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":316,"end":344},{"text":"But the key tip","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":346,"end":361},{"text":"would't","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":581,"end":588},{"text":"You can't concentrate on","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":467,"end":491},{"text":" you stick ","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":552,"end":563},{"text":" you would't think all the time "I need so much to do", because you will be more productive and satisfied with the list of completed tasks.
